content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


May 22, 2001




Richard D. Jones, City Attorney
City of Westminster
c/o Jones & Mayer
3777 North Harbor Boulevard
Fullerton, CA 92835

Re:     Your Request for Advice
        Our File No. A-01-113

Dear Mr. Jones:

        This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

        May a council member participate in council decisions to amend a General Plan
and change zoning where a participant in the proceeding is a campaign contributor to the
council member?

CONCLUSION

        Generally, the provisions set forth at Sections 87100 et seq. of the Act apply in
determining whether a city council member is precluded from participating in a given
council decision due to campaign contributions received. Section 84308 would not
prohibit his participation. In this case, there is no conflict of interest in the council
member participating in the decision concerning the General Plan amendment and zoning
change.



___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. A-01-113
Page No. 2

FACTS

A General Plan amendment ("GPA") and zone change is scheduled for a public
hearing at a city council meeting. A council member is concerned that he may have a
conflict of interest as a result of receiving campaign contributions from a part owner of
the property that is the subject of the GPA and zone change. Specifically, this council
member received and accepted monetary contributions each exceeding $250 within 12
months of the scheduled council meeting.

Your office has advised the council member that according to your interpretation
of Government Code section 84308, his participation would not constitute a conflict of
interest, and he should be allowed to participate in the meeting.

ANALYSIS

A council member is subject to the conflict-of-interest provisions that apply to
public officials in general. Those provisions are set forth at Section 87100 et seq.
Section 87100 states:

"No public official at any level of state or local government shall
make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his
official position to influence a governmental decision in which
he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest."

A public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision within the
meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision will
have a material financial effect on one or more of the public official's economic interests.
(§ 87103; Regulation 18700(a).) The Commission has adopted a standard analysis for
deciding whether an individual has a disqualifying conflict of interest in a given
governmental decision, which is applied here. (Regulation 18700, subd's. (b)(1)-(8).)
The Commission recently enacted changes in its regulatory scheme to determine where a
conflict may exist.

1.     Public official.

The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to "public officials."
(§§ 87100, 87103; Regulation 18700, subd. (b)(1).) "Public official" is defined as every
member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government agency.
(§ 82048.) A "local government agency" means a county, city or district of any kind,
including a school district, or any other local political subdivision, or any county board
commission. (§ 82041.) A member of the Westminster City Council is a "public
official," for purposes of the Act (see Sections 82048, 82041), and the conflict-of-interest
rules apply to the council member.

File No. A-01-113
Page No. 3

2. Will the council member be participating in a governmental decision?

The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions come into play only when a public
official makes, participates in making, or in some way attempts to use his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the official knows — or has
reason to know — that he or she has a financial interest. (Section 87100.) Commission
regulations describe in detail what constitutes making, participating in making, or
influencing a governmental decision. (Regulations 18702.1, 18702.2, and 18702.3,
respectively.) The council member clearly will be making, participating in making, or
influencing a governmental decision if he votes and deliberates on decisions regarding a
General Plan amendment and/or zoning changes.

3. What are the council member's economic interests?

The "economic interests" from which conflicts of interest may arise are described
by Section 87103 and Regulations 18703-18703.5. There are six kinds of economic
interests recognized under the Act. The pertinent economic interest at issue here is:

Any source of income (Section 87103(c))

Campaign contributions required to be reported under Chapter 4 of the Act are
expressly excluded from the definition of income (Section 82030 (b)(1)). Because the
amount of the contribution in question is reportable pursuant to Section 84211(c), it does
not meet the definition of income. Therefore, it does not appear that the council member
has an economic interest in the decisions in question.

Your question also raises the application of Section 84308. Section 84308
prohibits any officer of an agency from accepting, soliciting, or directing a contribution
of more than two hundred fifty dollars ($250) from any party, or his or her agent, or from
any participant, or his or her agent, while a proceeding involving a license, permit, or
other entitlement for use is pending before the agency, and for three months following the
date a final decision is rendered in the proceeding, if the officer knows or has reason to
know that the participant has a financial interest in the proceeding. City council members
who are directly elected by the voters are exempt from the provisions of Section 84308.
(Section 84308 (a)(3).) Your request for advice indicates that the voters directly elect the
council member; therefore, he is exempt from the provisions of Section 84308.

We have not found it necessary to address the remaining five steps in the analysis
because in this case, the council member has no economic interest from which a conflict
of interest would arise.²

² This letter is based on the facts as they have been presented to us. The Commission does not act as a
finder of fact in providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

File No. A-01-113
Page No. 4

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Jody Feldman
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

JF:jg

sections.question:
May a council member participate in council decisions to amend a General Plan
and change zoning where a participant in the proceeding is a campaign contributor to the
council member?
sections.conclusion:
Generally, the provisions set forth at Sections 87100 et seq. of the Act apply in
determining whether a city council member is precluded from participating in a given
council decision due to campaign contributions received. Section 84308 would not
prohibit his participation. In this case, there is no conflict of interest in the council
member participating in the decision concerning the General Plan amendment and zoning
change.

QUESTION: May a council member participate in council decisions to amend a General Plan
and change zoning where a participant in the proceeding is a campaign contributor to the
council member?

CONCLUSION: Generally, the provisions set forth at Sections 87100 et seq. of the Act apply in
determining whether a city council member is precluded from participating in a given
council decision due to campaign contributions received. Section 84308 would not
prohibit his participation. In this case, there is no conflict of interest in the council
member participating in the decision concerning the General Plan amendment and zoning
change.
